ucs.title: [LLM] Storm-1849 (ArcaneDoor / UAT4356) — lateral movement from Cisco ASA VPN pool after Line Dancer cookie theft
ucs.description: After Storm-1849 (Microsoft cluster name; tracked by Cisco Talos as UAT4356, campaign ArcaneDoor) exploits a Cisco ASA via CVE-2024-20353 / CVE-2024-20359 and deploys the Line Dancer in-memory shellcode loader + Line Runner persistence implant, the operators harvest active AnyConnect/WebVPN session cookies (T1539) and replay them to pivot into the internal estate from VPN-pool addresses. This UC fans-out detection on a single source IP from the VPN pool authenticating to N+ distinct internal hosts inside a short window with a privileged account — the hallmark post-ArcaneDoor pivot.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count, dc(Authentication.dest) AS hosts_touched, values(Authentication.dest) AS hosts, values(Authentication.signature_id) AS signature_ids, min(_time) AS first_seen, max(_time) AS last_seen FROM datamodel=Authentication WHERE Authentication.action=success AND (Authentication.signature_id=4624 OR Authentication.app="win:remote") BY Authentication.user, Authentication.src, _time span=30m | `drop_dm_object_name(Authentication)` | eval is_machine=if(match(user,"\$$"),1,0) | search is_machine=0 | where cidrmatch("10.200.0.0/16",src) OR cidrmatch("10.201.0.0/16",src) | where hosts_touched>=5 | sort - hosts_touched
ucs.kql:
let VpnPoolCidrs = dynamic(["10.200.0.0/16","10.201.0.0/16"]);   // <-- AnyConnect / WebVPN address pools
let Window  = 30m;
let MinHosts = 5;
let KnownAdminPivots = dynamic(["svc-vpngw","jumpbox-rdp"]);    // benign pivots, prune as needed
DeviceLogonEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "LogonSuccess"
| where LogonType in ("Network","RemoteInteractive","Interactive")
| where AccountName !endswith "$"                              // skip machine accounts
| where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
| where DeviceName !in~ (KnownAdminPivots)
| where ipv4_is_in_any_range(RemoteIP, VpnPoolCidrs)
| summarize HostsTouched = dcount(DeviceId),
            Hosts        = make_set(DeviceName, 25),
            LogonTypes   = make_set(LogonType),
            Protocols    = make_set(Protocol),
            FirstSeen    = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen     = max(Timestamp)
            by AccountName, RemoteIP, bin(Timestamp, Window)
| where HostsTouched >= MinHosts                               // single VPN IP, many internal hosts → fan-out
| order by HostsTouched desc, LastSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let VpnPoolCidrs = dynamic(["10.200.0.0/16","10.201.0.0/16"]);   // AnyConnect / WebVPN address pools
let Window  = 30m;
let MinHosts = 5;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4624                                         // successful logon
| where LogonType in (3, 10, 2)                                 // Network, RemoteInteractive, Interactive
| where AccountType == "User"
| where TargetUserName !endswith "$"                            // skip machine accounts
| where isnotempty(IpAddress) and IpAddress != "-"
| where ipv4_is_in_any_range(IpAddress, VpnPoolCidrs)
| summarize HostsTouched = dcount(Computer),
            Hosts        = make_set(Computer, 25),
            LogonTypes   = make_set(LogonTypeName),
            AuthPkgs     = make_set(AuthenticationPackageName),
            FirstSeen    = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen     = max(TimeGenerated)
            by TargetUserName, IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, Window)
| where HostsTouched >= MinHosts
| order by HostsTouched desc, LastSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Generic VPN-then-pivot detections do not target Storm-1849 — what does is the combination of (a) traffic originating from the corporate VPN pool, (b) using a privileged human account, and (c) fanning out to many internal hosts in a short window. ArcaneDoor's Line Dancer exists specifically to harvest live AnyConnect sessions and Line Runner ensures the WebVPN persistence survives reboot — both of

ucs.title: [LLM] Storm-1849 ArcaneDoor — Cisco ASA AAA tampering & syslog suppression (Line Dancer indicator-blocking)
ucs.description: Line Dancer hooks the ASA AAA module and disables/filters syslog (T1562.006 + T1562.001) before the operator runs commands so that crashinfo, AAA-auth, and connection-build messages disappear from the SIEM. Hunt the CEF feed coming off the ASA cluster for the specific message-IDs and config-write activity that ArcaneDoor leaves behind, plus a sudden drop in baseline %ASA-6-302* connection volume from the same chassis.
ucs.splunk: `cisco_asa` (eventtype=cisco_asa_config OR signature_id IN ("111008","111010","199019","505005","505007","509001","725007","716001","716002")) | rex field=_raw "(?i)(?<asa_action>no logging|disable logging|aaa authentication|aaa-server|webvpn|import webvpn|lua_clientupdate|client-services|crashinfo)" | where isnotnull(asa_action) | stats count, values(asa_action) AS actions, values(signature_id) AS sigs, values(user) AS users, values(src) AS src_ips, min(_time) AS first_seen, max(_time) AS last_seen by host | append [ search `cisco_asa` signature_id=302* earliest=-14d@d latest=-1d@d | bucket _time span=1h | stats count AS baseline_conn by host, _time | stats avg(baseline_conn) AS baseline_avg, stdev(baseline_conn) AS baseline_sd by host ] | append [ search `cisco_asa` signature_id=302* earliest=-1h@h | stats count AS recent_conn by host ] | stats values(*) AS * by host | eval anomaly=if(recent_conn < (baseline_avg - 3*baseline_sd),1,0) | where count>0 OR anomaly=1
ucs.kql:
// ArcaneDoor implants live in ASA memory — Defender XDR has no direct visibility on ASA control-plane.
// This companion query catches ENDPOINT-side staging: any internal host pushing config / SSH to the
// ASA management interface from a process other than the sanctioned admin tooling.
let AsaMgmtIPs = dynamic(["10.10.10.1","10.10.10.2"]);                  // ASA mgmt interfaces
let KnownMgmtBins = dynamic(["putty.exe","plink.exe","ssh.exe","mstsc.exe","ansible-playbook.exe","prime-infrastructure.exe","asdm-launcher.exe"]);
let KnownAdminHosts = dynamic(["jumpbox-net-01","jumpbox-net-02"]);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where RemoteIP in (AsaMgmtIPs)
| where RemotePort in (22, 443, 23)                                        // SSH / HTTPS-mgmt / Telnet
| where ActionType in ("ConnectionSuccess","ConnectionAttempt")
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| where DeviceName !in~ (KnownAdminHosts)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (KnownMgmtBins)
   or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","python.exe","curl.exe","rundll32.exe","mshta.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          RemoteIP, RemotePort, ActionType
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays  = 14d;
let RecentWindow  = 1h;
let AsaProducts   = dynamic(["ASA","Adaptive Security Appliance","ASA 5500","FTD","Firepower"]);
// 1) Suspicious config / AAA / WebVPN write activity on the ASA
let ConfigSignals =
    CommonSecurityLog
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where DeviceVendor =~ "Cisco" and DeviceProduct in~ (AsaProducts)
    | where DeviceEventClassID in ("111008","111010","199019","505005","505007","509001","725007","716001","716002")
         or Message has_any ("no logging","disable logging","aaa authentication","aaa-server",
                              "webvpn","import webvpn","lua_clientupdate","client-services",
                              "show memory region","show process","crashinfo")
    | extend Indicator = case(
        Message has "lua_clientupdate",                 "LineRunner_lua_persistence",
        Message has_any ("no logging","disable logging"),"LineDancer_logging_disable",
        Message has_any ("aaa authentication","aaa-server"),"AAA_modification",
        Message has_any ("webvpn","import webvpn","client-services"),"WebVPN_modification",
        Message has_any ("show memory region","crashinfo"),"Recon_or_AntiForensics",
        "Other")
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, DeviceProduct, DeviceEventClassID,
              SourceUserName, SourceIP, Indicator, Activity, Message;
// 2) Sudden collapse of normal connection-build telemetry on the same chassis (Line Dancer mutes syslog)
let Baseline =
    CommonSecurityLog
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(LookbackDays) .. ago(RecentWindow))
    | where DeviceVendor =~ "Cisco" and DeviceProduct in~ (AsaProducts)
    | where DeviceEventClassID startswith "302"
    | summarize HourlyCount = count() by Computer, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
    | summarize BaselineAvg = avg(HourlyCount), BaselineSd = stdev(HourlyCount) by Computer;
let Recent =
    CommonSecurityLog
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(RecentWindow)
    | where DeviceVendor =~ "Cisco" and DeviceProduct in~ (AsaProducts)
    | where DeviceEventClassID startswith "302"
    | summarize RecentCount = count() by Computer;
let TelemetryGap =
    Baseline
    | join kind=leftouter Recent on Computer
    | extend RecentCount = coalesce(RecentCount, 0)
    | where RecentCount < (BaselineAvg - 3 * BaselineSd) and BaselineAvg > 50
    | extend Indicator = "LineDancer_telemetry_blackout", TimeGenerated = now()
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Indicator, BaselineAvg, BaselineSd, RecentCount;
union ConfigSignals, TelemetryGap
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: ArcaneDoor's two implants are public-knowledge: Line Dancer mutates AAA + suppresses syslog from inside the ASA process; Line Runner uses the WebVPN client-services lua_clientupdate hook to survive reboot. Generic firewall-config-change rules will not catch this — you need to (a) look for the specific message-IDs and config keywords ArcaneDoor touches and (b) treat a sudden telemetry blackout from

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `Computer` is not a column of CommonSecurityLog; it does exist on SecurityEvent, WindowsEvent, Event, Syslog, AKSAudit, DnsEvents, W3CIISLog, Heartbeat, Update, ImProcessCreate.
